channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Rocket League Teaser Hints at Respawn Change Over 10 Years in the Making
item.content:encoded:
Rocket League has been a constant success, and millions of matches have been registered over a decade plus. Psyonix has pretty much kept the core gameplay the same, but a new teaser hints at the biggest gameplay change in 11 years.

Do you realise how solid and fun a live-service game has to be for the developer to not have to make many (if any) significant changes over a period of 10 years? Rocket League has nailed what it wants to be and what it needs to be for the community for over a decade.

Big changes are being teased for the sports/racer, and the game’s respawn mechanic might be getting an overhaul.

Rocket League Could be Adding Ability to Choose Respawn

In Rocket League, if you get demo’d—when an enemy player boosts into you at speed and blows you up—you respawn back at your goal. The major caveat is that you don’t get to decide which side of the goal you appear on.

This has drastic consequences for live gameplay. For example, you could spawn on the same side of the arena as the ball, making it easier to take possession, or you could spawn on the opposite side of the ball, which is dangerously near your goal, making it completely RNG if you can reach the ball in time and prevent a goal, etc.

Respawns have been a source of community frustration since the game launched in 2015, and it’s one of the few gripes I (and most RL fans) have with the game.

Psyonix gave everyone hope on May 22, with the release of a social media post titled: ‘The decision is in your control.’

The short clip showed a car getting demo’d and spawning on one side of the arena. The location leads to the opposition team scoring, but the footage quickly rewinds and shows a new UI prompt, indicating that you can now finally decide where to spawn!

With the announcement that Unreal Engine 6 will power the next era of Rocket League, new changes are being talked about. This would easily be one of the biggest and boldest updates Psyonix has ever made.

Check out the teaser below, as it might be time to get excited for Rocket League’s future.

Make sure you have the Insider Gaming Showcase marked down in your diary. It will feature 55 games, 12 World Premieres, new trailers, and much more that you don’t want to miss.

Talk about the change and Rocket League in general via our Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight DLC characters reportedly leaking and IO Interactive’s interesting response to 007: First Light’s opening mission leaking online for more news.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Elden Ring Movie Leaks Reveal New Set Footage
item.content:encoded:
New footage has surfaced online, revealing the Elden Ring live-action movie’s set in Wales.

The Elden Ring live-action movie has been shaping up well. We were recently informed that they secured a prominent actor from another highly popular game adaptation, HBO’s The Last of Us. Now, new footage shows us one of the sets that will be featured in the upcoming movie.

Spotted by CultureCrave on X (formerly Twitter), a video from mightbeliber’s TikTok account reveals the Elden Ring live-action set in Wales. It seems that one of the scenes from the upcoming movie is being shot at Conwy Castle, as we see different types of soldiers stationed at several vantage points of the castle. The soldiers are fully clad in knightly armor and wield a variety of weapons, including crossbows, swords, and more. That’s all that was revealed in the footage.

The official announcement for the live-action adaptation of Elden Ring arrived last month, revealing that Bandai Namco Entertainment and A24 will produce the movie. The movie will be written and directed by Alex Garland and will be filmed for IMAX. The release date has also been confirmed for March 3, 2028, and the cast includes Kit Connor, Ben Whishaw, Tom Burke, Nick Offerman, and more.

In other news, Elden Ring’s movie director, Alex Garland, is a pro at Elden Ring after a series of playthroughs that have hardened his resolve. Additionally, enthusiastic fans will be able to learn more about the game’s lore through a 400-page book. What are your thoughts on the leaked set photos from the Elden Ring live-action movie in Wales? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about how 007 First Light’s developers have added a major TikTok star to the cast. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Faker and T1 Qualify For LoL Esports World Cup 2026
item.content:encoded:
Reigning League of Legends World Champions T1 have qualified for the Esports World Cup after defeating Dplus Kia in the Korean qualifiers.

T1 had to settle for third in last year’s Esports World Cup and will be hoping to add yet another trophy to their collection.

T1 glided through the Korean qualifiers, sweeping both HANJIN BRION and BNK FEARX before facing DK in the Upper Bracket final.

An amazing comeback saw T1 close out the series and win 3-1 to qualify for the $2 million tournament later this year.

It looked like the series would go to five games as 31 minutes into Game 4, DK were 6k gold up and on Soul point.

Yet, just seven minutes later, T1 had won the series.

A huge flash Tibbers by Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok helped T1 win the teamfight at the next Drake, securing the team a lot of bounty gold.

Then in the next fight, a fantastic engage by Mun ‘Oner’ Hyeon-jun earned T1 a clean ace and the series.

The series made Oner’s omission from Korea’s Asian Games LoL 2026 roster even more baffling.

DK will now face Hanwha Life Esports in Korea’s other EWC Qualifier match.

Esports World Cup Qualified LoL Teams

  • Gen.G
  • T1
  • Anyone’s Legend
  • JD Gaming
  • G2 Esports
  • Karmine Corp
  • Team Secret
  • Sentinels

The online qualifiers for EWC LoL 2026 have almost concluded, with half of the spots now filled in this year’s expanded 16-team tournament.

Gen.G automatically qualified after winning last year’s EWC title and will be joined by T1, the winner of HLE vs DK, and the eventual LCK Road to MSI Champion.

From the LPL, Anyone’s Legend and JDG secured their places through China’s online qualifiers and will be joined by the LPL Split 2 Playoffs winners.

Meanwhile, G2 Esports and Karmine Corp won the EMEA EWC qualifiers and will be joined by the eventual LEC Spring Playoffs champions.

Sentinels won the NA qualifiers while Team Secret qualified from APAC.

That then leaves slots for the eventual APAC, LCS, and CBLoL champions, as well as one Brazilian qualifier winner.

Who else do you expect to qualify for EWC this year? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see why the Gen.G CEO criticised KeSPA’s Asia Games LoL team selection process.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: 007: First Light Reveals Major TikTok Star As Part of Cast
item.content:encoded:
You might cringe at a TikTok content creator being added to 007: First Light, but based on what I’m seeing, it looks like a tiny cameo, and it’s at least one of the best creators on the platform.

The 007: First Light cast was revealed, showing all voice actors. This is just the main cast, though, meaning there are still some bit-part roles being saved for the game’s main credits.

But if you play through 007: First Light, there’s a good chance you’ll recognize one of social media’s most iconic stars.

TikTok Star Khaby Lame Joins 007: First Light Cast

Khaby Lame is in 007: First Light!

I bet that’s a sentence you didn’t think you’d read today. It’s true: TikTok sensation Khaby Lame has managed to sweet-talk his way into the new Bond game.

Lame became a viral sensation with his incredible TikTok videos, where he reacted very deadpan to complicated TikTok tutorial videos and more. His hand gesture and stoic face instantly became endearing, and it helped the social media star accumulate a seismic following on TikTok.

So much so that in 2026, he’s still reportedly the most-followed TikTok account, sitting at over 150 million followers.

He’s broadened his horizons since and has had an Italian voice cameo in Black Panther: Wakanda Forever, and a tiny cameo in Bad Boys: Ride or Die. Well, it’s time to add 007: First Light to the list.

The official X page revealed Lame’s cameo, assisting James Bond with his trademark gesture and face. It’s dumb, but it’s harmless fun, and marks another huge milestone for the social media personality.

The Insider Gaming Showcase debuts on May 28, featuring 55 games, 12 World Premieres, new trailers, and much more that you don’t want to miss. Find out all the details in the link.

Does this addition help or hinder 007: First Light? Are you happy for the TikTok influencer? Share your thoughts with us through our Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight DLC characters reportedly leaking and IO Interactive’s interesting response to 007: First Light’s opening mission leaking online for more news.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

item.title: PlayStation Patent Could Address Loading Screen Boredom
item.content:encoded:
A new PlayStation patent has surfaced online, which aims to offer picture-in-picture content for loading screens, making wait times less boring.

We frequently see new Sony patents surface online, aimed at further innovation in the gaming space. Most recently, we saw a patent for a controller that doesn’t feature any physical buttons. We also saw a patent for AI tech that could essentially play the game for you. Now, their new target is loading screens.

PlayStation Patent Could Revive Picture-in-Picture Function

Spotted by MP1ST, the new PlayStation patent talks about offering picture-in-picture content for loading screens. The goal of these patents is to make the loading screen experience in multiplayer titles more engaging for players, allowing them to view different types of content while waiting for their matches to begin.

Players will notice a new box appearing on their loading screen while the match is in progress. This box can feature a variety of content, including videos from linked accounts and applications, AI-generated gameplay highlights, AI-generated reports about upcoming opponents, videos from social media, advertisements, gameplay tips, and more.

The patent could definitely make loading screens a lot more engaging and interesting for PlayStation players, as they will be able to interact with the content while waiting for the match to begin. However, loading screens are shorter in most games, and in half of them, matches may begin before the player can interact with the loading screen content. We will have to see how PlayStation implements this and how useful it actually is, if and when the feature is released.

In other news, Sony has trademarked a new PlayStation game called ‘Break In’ ahead of its State of Play event in June. Sony has confirmed that a new PlayStation State of Play will arrive next month, featuring an extended look at Marvel’s Wolverine. What are your thoughts PlayStation’s new loading screen patent? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the 007 First Light’s first mission leaking online. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: IO Interactive Has Interesting Response to 007: First Light’s Opening Mission Leaking Online
item.content:encoded:
Physical copies of 007: First Light are already in the hands of aspiring agents, and as is always the case when this happens, some of its content has already made its way to the internet. This includes 007: First Light’s opening introductory mission, and developer IO Interactive has acknowledged the leak and even fired back.

007: First Light has been a long time coming, with the original announcement going as far back as 2020.

After six years in development and more, IO Interactive has a new series in its portfolio. There are elements of Hitman here, but it’s very much ‘Bond,’ and if you want to see it in action for yourself before the worldwide launch, you can—officially, too!

007: First Light’s First Mission Leaked in Full

The opening mission of 007: First Light quickly circulated online after a player uploaded the entire opening mission to YouTube.

It was discovered, though, that only the first mission is playable, with the rest of the game requiring an online download. Meaning the disc is pretty much an empty conduit, a frequent occurrence in modern gaming.

However, if you try to watch the original YouTube video, you’ll see that it’s been struck down, with a message saying: ‘This video is no longer available due to a copyright claim by IO Interactive A/S.’

I did say that IO has responded, and the developer has done so with a YouTube video of its own.

‘The First 13 Minutes of 007 First Light’ can be found on IO Interactive’s official YouTube channel. It’s exactly as advertised. The description has this to say:

‘Since a few users got hold of the disc early, watch the first 13 minutes of the opening mission in 007 First Light, as intended on day 1. Spoilers ahead, so if you’d rather save it for the in-game experience, best steer clear!’

To reiterate, this is the first 13 minutes of gameplay, and spoilers are inevitable. If you want to save the opening minutes for yourself, wait until launch.

If not, check out the footage; who knows, it might sell you on the game!

The Insider Gaming Showcase is around the corner—on May 28, to be exact. There will be 55 games, including 12 World Premieres, new trailers, and much more that you don’t want to miss.

What do you think of 007: First Light’s opening mission leaking? Share your thoughts on it through the always-free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight DLC characters reportedly leaking.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

item.title: Fallout: Siege of New Vegas Trailer Drops Action-Packed Reveal
item.content:encoded:
Fallout: Siege of New Vegas might just be the most impeccable piece of post-apocalyptic media I’ve seen in years. It has been a few days since the first teaser trailer for Fallout: Siege of New Vegas was released online, and in that time, it has been turning heads, racking up tens of thousands of views, but deserving so much more!

It’s only one minute long, but the first teaser for Siege of New Vegas gives us the best impression possible of what a Fallout: New Vegas remaster might look like, if it’s ever made.

Fallout: Siege of Vegas Stuns in New Trailer

Earlier today, I stumbled across the first teaser trailer for Fallout: Siege of New Vegas, a fan-made film built in Unreal Engine to celebrate the incredibly popular 2010 post-apocalyptic RPG from Obsidian.

This trailer is superb, and it comes courtesy of DINZHI, a YouTube creator with more than 75,000 subscribers.

It’s a fan-made experience that is set after the NCR ending of Fallout: New Vegas, which sees the player character assist with the New California Republic’s dominance over Caesar’s Legion at the Battle of Hoover Dam. In the description, it states:

The fan-film main storyline will heavily focus on BOS invasion of NCR controlled Mojave wasteland.

Here’s the full teaser for Fallout: Siege of New Vegas:

It’s pretty well done, even if there are some canonical inconsistencies that some die-hard fans might have issues with, such as the power armor models used in the reveal.

I’m going to be closely following this one, as I’m a sucker for anything related to Fallout.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’re excited for more Fallout content to eventually surface.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that LEGO Batman DLC might have leaked

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: IEM Cologne 2026 Major Stage 1 Preview: Pick’Ems Predictions
item.content:encoded:
The IEM Cologne 2026 Major is set to start on June 2, with Stage 1 starting three weeks of Counter-Strike action, which will end with the first Major champion crowned at the Lanxess Arena in a decade.

Stage 1 is always the least predictable part of any Major, with Best of 1 contests starting the biggest tournament of the Counter-Strike calendar, so Insider Gaming has a look at who is likely to progress to Stage 2.

The opening matches for the IEM Cologne 2026 Major are:

  • GamerLegion vs NRG
  • B8 vs TYLOO
  • Heroic vs Sharks
  • BetBoom vs Gaimin Gladiators
  • BIG vs Liquid
  • M80 vs Lynn Vision
  • MIBR vs THUNDER dOWNUNDER
  • Sinners vs FlyQuest

Who will progress to Stage 2 of the IEM Cologne 2026 Major?

3-0 Predictions: GamerLegion & BetBoom

GamerLegion is one of the most in-form teams in the world, believe it or not. The roster has only lost to NAVI or Vitality since returning to the Tier 1 sphere at BLAST Fort Worth, losing once to Vitality at that event, and losing three times to NAVI at BLAST, and IEM Atlanta, where they finished second.

Under Janusz ‘Snax’ Pogorzelski, GamerLegion’s rifling core of Fredrik ‘REZ’ Sterner, Oldřich ‘PR’ Nový, and Sebastian ‘Tauson’ Lindelof seem to be back to their best, but what is even better is that AWPer Milan ‘hypex’ Polowiec has blossomed into a clutching star in their North American event swing.

In that time, the roster has defeated Furia, Astralis (twice), Sinners, Liquid, paiN, and CAC 2026 Champions Legacy. Overall, that list of teams far outweighs the threat that Stage 1 should pose.

BetBoom doesn’t have quite the same form, but showed an excellent level in the Cologne qualification run-in, defeating G2, Heroic, and GamerLegion on the way to securing enough VRS points to make the Major.

At IEM Atlanta, the roster upset Vitality, displaying controlled chaos to leave Vitality uncomfortable on the server. Despite losing in the Semis and the Third/Fourth place decider, the roster’s results show they can go deeper in the event than just Stage 1.

Notably, BetBoom defeated B8 2-1 in their opening match at IEM Atlanta. Depending on other results, the two may meet again in the 2-0 matchups in Stage 1.

3-1 Predictions: B8, mibr, Heroic

B8 feels like a fairly safe bet to make Stage 2 of the major after a season that has seen the roster have several relatively positive finishes in Tier 1, making the playoffs of CAC and PGL Bucharest 2026.

The roster has fully recovered from the unexpected departure of Danyyl ‘headtr1ck’ Valitov, and while Danylo ‘s1zzi’ Vinnyk’s level is still in question, the team’s overall level is largely not.

mibr comes into the Major as one of the most in-form Stage 1 teams, after a 4th place finish at the CS Asia Championships. The roster’s semi-international experiment also saw them reach the playoffs of PGL Bucharest, but most importantly, they still have star rifler Felipe ‘insani’ Yuji to carry them when times get tough.

Heroic initially pulled off some strong showings after the unfortunate benching of Älımjan ‘Alkaren’ Bıtımbai due to persistent Visa issues. With Simon ‘yxngstxr’ Boije AWPing, the team has beaten Monte, Parivision, NIP, and Aurora.

However, PGL Astana 2026 was a worrying result for the roster, losing to Gentle Mates and magic, teams the roster should be beating as Major qualifiers. They still seem strong enough to go through, but are not as definite as they once were.

3-2 Predictions: BIG, M80, Liquid

There’s a lot to appreciate about BIG. The roster has enjoyed a rebirth under ambitious signing Benjamin ‘blameF Bremer, with AWPer Gleb ‘gr1ks’ Gazin also recapturing some of the potential that saw him move to Heroic.

The main issue with BIG is their lack of Tier 1 credentials, largely taking the easiest possible route to the Major through local LANs. Are wins against 100 Thieves, magic, and Sharks enough to show that they can sneak them into Stage 2? Possibly…

M80 has looked a shadow of their 2025 selves for much of the year, with defeat to Liquid at IEM Atlanta encapsulating the roster’s fall from NA supremacy. Still, the roster’s DraculaN Season 6 win saw them beat Liquid, Aurora, and Monte on their way to the Major. If M80 can tap into some of what makes them tick, they should make Stage 2.

The final spot may come down to a toss-up between TYLOO and Liquid. TYLOO usually save their best for the Major, though that did not materialize in Budapest. This time around, the roster is not even dominant in their region, but still has shown some potential, defeating MOUZ in a Bo1 at CAC.

For Liquid, the roster seems so hopeless and devoid of life. Changes will surely come to the team, who are only at the Major due to a change of region back to NA, but with Roland ‘ultimate’ Tomkowiak performing better at CAC, Liquid only need a rifler or two to come online to make Stage 2.

Despite all the misgivings about this languishing Liquid roster, and despite all the instincts of this writer, Liquid seem like they might just be able to put three wins together and progress.

Who will bomb out?

0-3 Predictions: THUNDER dOWNUNDER & FlyQuest

It has become somewhat of a meme to pick the Australian team to go 0-3 in the Pick’Ems just for them to get a win or two and ruin people’s chances of getting the Diamond Coin.

There are two Aussie teams at the IEM Cologne Major, so that’s either twice the opportunity to be right about a 0-3 pick, or twice the opportunity to have your Pick’Ems ruined.

It may surprise a few readers to learn that THUNDER dOWNUNDER are the higher VRS-ranked roster of the two, but have achieved that largely by farming Australian events.

As for FlyQuest, the roster has put more money into playing Asian events, also taking part in DracuLan Season 5. Those events have, without exception, been failures to one degree or another, recently losing to lowly ranked rosters 5star and JiJieHao to come 9th-12th at the Hero Esports Asian Champions League.

The Australian teams seem relatively weak in comparison to EU, NA, and even Asian rosters at the Major, making them a natural choice for 0-3 records at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major.

Let us know who you think will progress from Stage 1 on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
